name: Windsurf Editor (Codeium)
what_it_does: Codeium's AI-native code editor with 'Cascade' agent mode. Features deep codebase understanding, multi-step agent-driven coding flows, and MCP server integration for extended tool access.
use_cases: Long-horizon coding tasks with Cascade agent mode (multi-step plans executed autonomously)
AI-assisted coding with awareness of entire codebase via deep indexing
Connecting to external tools via MCP servers within the editor
Collaborative AI code generation with explicit approval gates
Migrating or refactoring large codebases with agent assistance
not_for: Teams requiring strict on-premise AI (code sent to cloud)
best_when: Developers wanting Cursor-style AI coding with a different underlying model (Codeium's own + third-party). Cascade agent mode is strong for multi-step autonomous coding tasks.
avoid_when: Sensitive codebases with strict data handling requirements, or when you need guaranteed Anthropic Claude models exclusively.

auth.notes: Codeium account required. Sign in via GitHub, Google, or email. MCP servers configured globally or per-workspace.

paid_tiers.name: Pro
paid_tiers.features: Unlimited Cascade flows, priority access, premium models

pricing.notes: Free tier includes 25 Cascade agentic flows/month and unlimited code completions. Pro at $15/month is more cost-effective than Cursor Pro at $20/month.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: MCP integration is functional but documentation is less mature than Cursor's. Cascade agent mode has good error recovery for coding tasks.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cascade plans may have side effects — file writes, terminal commands. Approval gates help prevent unintended idempotency violations.
known_agent_gotchas: MCP support is newer than Cursor's — some tool call patterns may behave differently
Cascade agent mode requires explicit approval by default — fully autonomous mode is opt-in
Context window for Cascade is large but finite — very large codebases may be partially indexed

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. Code processed in cloud — same caution as Cursor for sensitive repos. Codeium has enterprise privacy commitments but verify for regulated industries.
